overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto has a significant social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, totaling 3580 followers. Engagement varies by platform, with LinkedIn demonstrating strong interaction through likes and comments, while Bluesky sees notable reposts. Content primarily focuses on research, job opportunities, and conference presentations related to computational social science and disinformation.

contentThemes.description: Posts about research projects, publications, and methodologies in computational social science.
Content related to disinformation, coordinated inauthentic behavior, and information operations on social media platforms.
Announcements for postdoctoral researchers and PhD students in related fields.
Promotion of upcoming presentations and participation in relevant conferences.

recommendations: Cross-promote content across platforms to maximize reach and engagement.
Use platform-specific hashtags to increase visibility.
Engage with comments and replies to foster a stronger community.
Experiment with different content formats (e.g., videos, images) to diversify engagement.
Post consistently to maintain audience interest.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows a strong interest in research publications and opportunities. Reposts are more frequent than on other platforms, indicating content is shared widely within the community.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon engagement is lower compared to other platforms. Content related to team releases and discussions receives moderate interaction.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn posts receive the highest engagement through likes and comments, particularly related to grant announcements and job postings. Professional networking appears to be a key driver.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
